description: Aggregate index generated from papers/<PaperName>/status.json. Paper-local status files are the source of truth for status, human summaries, review rows, and PaperInterface metadata.
review_count_policy: reviewed_rows counts saved human dashboard rows tracked in the public repository. total_rows counts the current dashboard review surface from each paper-local status.json. Agent source audits are not counted as human review.
paper_interface_maintenance_policy: PaperInterface.lean should stay compact and source-facing. Broad proof aliases belong in ProofInterface.lean or implementation modules.

papers.title: Addressing Discretization-Induced Bias in Demographic Prediction
papers.authors: Evan Dong, Aaron Schein, Yixin Wang, and Nikhil Garg
papers.source_version: ACM FAccT 2024 / PNAS Nexus 2025 published version
papers.main_caveat: The strengthened transitive provenance audit finds no unresolved certificate/source-boundary dependency for the current paper-facing status. Explicit assumptions remain source-matched, including the human-verified implicit multiclass condition.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: User-item fairness tradeoffs in recommendations
papers.authors: Sophie Greenwood, Sudalakshmee Chiniah, and Nikhil Garg
papers.main_caveat: Strict transitive provenance audit boundary: LP/duality and feasible-support optimality certificates for the Problem 6/11 reduction and misestimation tradeoff rows remain source-boundary proof obligations.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

llm_assumption_review.policy: Every paper-facing theorem premise not derived from prior Lean declarations must be declared in Assumptions.lean, listed in assumption_names, and judged by an independent LLM as a true paper/source model assumption or paper-statement condition rather than a proof assumption.

papers.title: Who is in Your Top Three? Optimizing Learning in Elections with Many Candidates
papers.authors: Nikhil Garg, Lodewijk Gelauff, Sukolsak Sakshuwong, Ashish Goel
papers.main_caveat: Strict transitive provenance audit boundary: finite-large-deviation/Cramer, exponential-rate, pairwise score-gap, and randomized pivotal-pair certificates remain reusable proof-boundary obligations.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: Competitive Auctions and Digital Goods
papers.authors: Andrew V. Goldberg, Jason D. Hartline, and Andrew Wright
papers.source_version: SODA 2001 paper; Theorem 8.2 is checked against the later Goldberg-Hartline-Karlin-Saks-Wright journal monotone-auction formulation.
papers.main_caveat: Strict transitive provenance audit boundary: The deterministic-truthfulness lower-bound endpoint still depends on the anonymous auction-family certificate for Theorem 9.3.
papers.human_summary: Formalizes the SODA paper; Theorem 8.2 uses the refined monotone-auction wording from the journal version.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: Designing Informative Rating Systems: Evidence from an Online Labor Market
papers.source_version: arXiv 1810.13028 TeX/PDF cache; publication citation: Manufacturing & Service Operations Management 23(3):589-605, published online 2020 (issue 2021)
papers.main_caveat: Strict transitive provenance audit boundary: the rating-system large-deviation route still depends on finite Chernoff/Cramer, exponential-rate, and pairwise threshold-rate certificates.
papers.human_summary: Theorem 1 is formalized under the support-safe finite-rate convention from ordinal upper-tail dominance and full finite ordinal rating support.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: Driver Surge Pricing
papers.main_caveat: Strict transitive provenance audit boundary: the compact endpoint still reaches boundary interval, a.e. policy-shape, optimizer-replacement, and sequential dynamic-reward certificates; the denominator-valid defined-reward theorem is formalized, but the full certificate provenance is not yet derived from primitives.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: College Admissions and the Stability of Marriage
papers.authors: D. Gale and L. S. Shapley
papers.main_caveat: Strict transitive provenance audit boundary: the Gale-Shapley existence/optimality rows still depend on deferred-acceptance invariant and stability certificates in the shared matching library.
papers.human_summary: This only uses a few lines of code as its infrastructure has largely been elevated to the shared matching library.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

llm_assumption_review.policy: Every paper-facing theorem premise not derived from prior Lean declarations must be declared in Assumptions.lean, listed in assumption_names, and judged by an independent LLM as a true paper/source model assumption rather than a proof assumption.

papers.title: Test-optional Policies: Overcoming Strategic Behavior and Informational Gaps
papers.main_caveat: Strict transitive provenance audit boundary: the test-optional endpoints still depend on Gaussian tail/hazard and source-equilibrium certificates rather than fully derived Gaussian source-model algebra.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: On Approximately Fair Allocations of Indivisible Goods
papers.authors: Richard J. Lipton, Evangelos Markakis, Elchanan Mossel, and Amin Saberi
papers.main_caveat: Strict transitive provenance audit boundary: the Section 3 runtime/search layer and some rounded-IP/counterexample endpoints remain certificate boundaries; Sections 2 and 4 stay formalized at their existing boundary.
papers.human_summary: Sections 2 and 4 are fully formalized. Section 3 has query/descent/rounded-search support. The PTAS/FPTAS runtime layer needs reusable fixed-dimension IP complexity infrastructure.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: Truth Revelation in Approximately Efficient Combinatorial Auctions
papers.authors: Daniel Lehmann, Liadan Ita O'Callaghan, and Yoav Shoham
papers.main_caveat: Strict transitive provenance audit boundary: the external complexity consequences, critical-value infinity axioms, and greedy-order blocking windows remain explicit partial boundaries.
papers.human_summary: Greedy approximation, truthfulness, and Theorem 6.1 reductions are formalized. Full formalization requires computational complexity results that are out of scope.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: Balancing Producer Fairness and Efficiency via Prior-Weighted Rating System Design
papers.authors: Thomas Ma, Michael S. Bernstein, Ramesh Johari, and Nikhil Garg
papers.main_caveat: The strengthened transitive provenance audit finds no unresolved certificate/source-boundary dependency for the current paper-facing status; the documented MBJG caveat remains the zero-denominator/utility-domain boundary already described in the report.
papers.human_summary: Formalization required an interior-quality assumption (0 < q_v < 1) for the strict variance-decrease statement.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

llm_assumption_review.policy: Every paper-facing theorem premise not derived from prior Lean declarations must be declared in Assumptions.lean, listed in assumption_names, and judged by an independent LLM as a paper/source model assumption, paper-statement condition, or documented caveat rather than a proof assumption.

papers.title: AdWords and Generalized Online Matching
papers.authors: Aranyak Mehta, Amin Saberi, Umesh Vazirani, and Vijay V. Vazirani
papers.main_caveat: Strict transitive provenance audit boundary: the Balance/MSVV finite-error and extensions still depend on approximation-accounting, state-invariant, and factor-revealing LP certificate APIs.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: Reconciling the Accuracy-Diversity Trade-off in Recommendations
papers.authors: Kenny Peng, Manish Raghavan, Emma Pierson, Jon Kleinberg, and Nikhil Garg
papers.source_version: The ACM Web Conference, 2024, pp. 1318--1329; arXiv:2307.15142v1
papers.main_caveat: Strict transitive provenance audit boundary: the Top-K oracle, Bernoulli tail/Laplace, rounding, averaging, and integral-asymptotic certificates remain explicit partial boundaries.
papers.human_summary: Proposition 2's printed finite bound appears to miss a factor of 2; Lean proves the corrected finite bound, which is sufficient for the asymptotic 1/2-homogeneity result. Fully formalizing the remaining result, Proposition 4, requires a general Laplace-principle-related analysis library.

human_summary_review.note: Do not rewrite this summary without explicit human instruction.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs
